original_abstract:
SimpleITK is an open-source interface for the Insight Segmentation and Registration Toolkit (ITK) designed to streamline rapid prototyping, education, and scientific data analysis across multiple high-level programming languages. While ITK is a powerful, templated C++ library known for its flexibility in biomedical image processing, its steep learning curve—driven by complex C++ templates and a demand-driven pipeline—often limits its accessibility to domain scientists and students. Previous attempts to wrap ITK, such as WrapITK, provided a direct mapping of this complex interface, resulting in large binaries and a steep barrier to entry for non-ITK experts.

In contrast, SimpleITK provides a simplified, non-templated "facade" layer that hides ITK’s internal complexities while maintaining its high-performance capabilities. Key features include a procedural interface, the abandonment of the complex pipeline in favor of immediate execution, and practical user conveniences like overloaded operators and binary distribution packages. To manage the vast array of ITK pixel types and image dimensions, SimpleITK utilizes a manual description of each filter combined with advanced C++ meta-programming and automated code generation. This architecture allows users to manipulate images across languages like Python, R, Java, and C# without managing memory or compile-time types. Licensed under the Apache License 2.0, SimpleITK aims to lower the barrier to entry for state-of-the-art image analysis, fostering more reproducible and accessible computational science. More information and downloads are available at http://www.simpleitk.org.
humanized_abstract:
SimpleITK is an open-source, high-level interface to the Insight Segmentation and Registration Toolkit (ITK). SimpleITK is good for rapid development, learning and science applications. The Insight Segmentation and Registration Toolkit (ITK) is a widely used, open-source C++ library for processing biomedical images. While very flexible and extremely powerful, ITK has a number of barriers to use. The C++ templates are notoriously difficult to understand and the demand driven processing of the pipeline can be unclear to non-experts. Several other attempts have been made to “wrap” the ITK, in the form of a direct one-to-one mapping from the ITK API to the new API. However, these have resulted in quite large binary sizes, and are also not necessarily easy to understand for people not familiar with the ITK.

ITK C++ Classes and Source Code The vast amount of classes and source code that is required to implement all of the processing capabilities in ITK make the use of the classes and source code very cumbersome. In SimpleITK we provide a facade interface that hides the complexity of the ITK classes, while at the same time maintaining the computational performance. We implemented this facade interface using a procedural interface style, instead of the pipeline interface provided by ITK, and by executing the processing directly on the objects, instead of building up a processing pipeline. Additionally, we used overloading of operators for a simpler syntax and used the .zip distribution provided by zip package tools. The large number of classes and filters provided by ITK leads to a large number of possible pixel types and number of dimensions that can be used for the processing. To describe the filters used in SimpleITK, we provide a manual listing of the filters and at the same time use some advanced features of C++ and meta-programming with code generation to reduce some of the tedium. In this way, users can process images in different programming languages, such as Python, R, Java and C# without having to worry about memory, or about the compile time type information. SimpleITK is released under the Apache License 2.0, in order to lower the barriers to applying the latest advances in image analysis to the development of computational science with more frequently reproducible research and the more open sharing of knowledge. More information and down loads can be found on the SimpleITK WebSite at http://www.simpleitk.org.
